description_long: This is a traditional hand axe featuring a robust, dark wooden handle with a natural grain pattern and a slightly curved ergonomic shape for improved grip. The handle appears to be made from a single piece of wood, smoothly finished but showing some wear and subtle textural variation. The axe head is forged from dark, matte metal, with a broad, wedge-shaped blade on one side and a flat poll on the opposite end for striking. The blade edge is straight and sharp, with a slight flare at the bottom, typical of utility axes used for chopping wood or general tasks. The head is securely mounted onto the handle using a traditional wedged fitting, with no visible screws or modern fasteners. There are no visible markings, engravings, or labels on either the handle or the blade.
description: A traditional hand axe with a dark wooden handle and a broad, matte metal blade, featuring a classic wedge-shaped design and ergonomic grip.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the wooden handle and the broad, wedge-shaped metal blade.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the rectangular cross-section of the handle and the thickness of the axe head.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's grain and the flat poll of the axe head.
description_view_3: Edge-on view showing the sharpness of the blade and the narrow profile of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, ergonomic wooden handle with a smooth finish and visible grain, oval cross-section, designed for comfortable grip.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad wedge-shaped blade with a straight edge and slight flare, flat poll on the opposite side, no decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged fitting securing the blade to the wooden handle.
